response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this embedded networking security role. There is no evidence of native-level Japanese proficiency (a hard requirement), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and no background in L2/L3 switching, MACsec, 802.1X, or embedded firmware for networking products. C experience is limited to a brief 7-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago; the career since has centered on TypeScript/Node.js web platforms.
strengths: Broad, long-tenured software engineering career with strong testing and craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some low-level and systems exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux familiarity
Demonstrated ownership of complete products end-to-end, including open-source platform work
concerns: No indication of Japanese language ability — a non-negotiable requirement for this role
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security-protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF)
C for UNIX and Linux kernel modification skills are absent or stale; recent work is web-focused TypeScript/Node

key_factors.factor: General engineering seniority and systems exposure
